response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced staff-level engineer with nearly two decades of professional development, which makes him significantly overqualified for an entry-level junior position. His core expertise lies in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than Java, which appears only peripherally in his history and skills list. Critically, the role requires strong verbal and written German, while his German is listed as basic. Salary expectations, seniority mismatch, and retention risk make this a poor fit despite his strong technical background.
strengths: Extensive full-stack experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, agile, pairing)
Proven communicator — runs workshops, talks, and training courses
concerns: Vastly overqualified for an entry-level junior role; high retention and salary-mismatch risk
Only basic German, while the role requires strong verbal and written German
Java is not a primary skill — most experience is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang

key_factors.factor: Seniority mismatch (staff engineer vs. junior role)
Solid engineering fundamentals and agile experience
